summary: Nvidia is demonstrating incredible growth and it's on the cusp of a huge opportunity . It's releasing new products to keep its dominant position in AI chips . As of this writing, Nvidia stock trades just north of $150 .  Nvidia has between 70% and 95% of the market, depending on who you ask . As the gold standard, Nvidia is relied upon by the biggest companies likeMicrosoftandAmazonas a partner .  Nvidia continues to report phenomenal results despite serious setbacks . Revenue increased 69% year over year, and earnings per share were up from $0.60 last year . Management continues to launch new products that can handle higher data loads .  Management is guiding for growth to decelerate in the second quarter but remain high at a 50% increase year over year . Wall Street is expecting $200 billion in revenue and $4.29 in EPS for the full year . Nvidia stock trades at a price-to-earnings ratio of 50 . If the valuation remains constant, Nvidia stock will rise along with earnings .  The likelihood is that Nvidia hits at least $200 before the end of the year, or shoots 27% higher than the price at the time of this writing . The Motley Fool's total average return is 1,069% — a market-crushing outperformance compared to177% for the S&P 500 .  The Motley Fool recommends Amazon, Microsoft, and Nvidia . Nvidia stock could reach $200 this year, according to Jennifer Saibil .

SoundHound AI's revenue is growing at an incredible year-over-year rate of 151%. However, the company is very small and lacks profits and positive free cash flow . The automotive and restaurant sectors remain key drivers of SoundHounds's growth .  Five of the eight sell-side analysts covering SoundHound stock rate it as a "strong buy" or "buy" The average analyst price target is $11.81, implying19% upside as of this writing .  SoundHound isn't yet profitable, and, crucially, it doesn't generate positive free cash flow . The AI ecosystem is extremely dynamic, with deep-pocketed companies eagerly jumping into new businesses . The company has a solid balance sheet, with more than $245 million in cash and only $5 million in debt .  SoundHound is a young company in a dynamic and fast-changing industry . However, it's not a stock for every investor . Its high valuation puts it well out of reach for value-minded investors . For growth-seeking investors who are looking for a mid-cap stock with plenty of upside .  The Motley Fool's total average return is 1,069% — a market-crushing outperformance compared to177% for the S&P 500 . Consider when Netflixmade this list on December 17, 2004... if you invested $1,000 at the time of our recommendation,you’d have $722,181!  SoundHound AI is a self-described AI AI company . The Motley Fool has a few of its own members of its board of directors . John Mackey is the former CEO of Whole Foods Market, an Amazon subsidiary .
